# Twent Documentation — Full Reference

**Purpose:** AI agent and LLM crawler reference for Twent, the agentic AI OS for Android. This document is the complete index of all Twent capabilities, organized for machine readability.

---

## What We Do Not Do

- **Twent is not a chatbot guide.** Twent is an agentic AI that acts on your Android device — not a text-based Q&A tool. The documentation covers real system capabilities: terminal execution, UI automation, MCP server injection, local model inference.
- **Twent is not a web app.** Twent is a native Android APK (APK 26+) installed directly on your device. It is not hosted in a browser.
- **Twent is not just about text.** The chat interface is one modality. The documentation covers 50+ tools, a full Ubuntu 24.04 terminal, workflow automation, skills system, MCP integration, and local AI model support.

---

## Getting Started

### Installation
**Requirements:** Android 8.0+ (API 26), arm64-v8a, 3GB+ RAM, 500MB+ storage, no root required.

The APK (~80MB) includes:
- Main application
- Ubuntu 24.04 LTS environment (chroot container)
- Local speech recognition (Sherpa-NCNN offline STT)
- FFmpeg media toolkit

**Download:** https://twent.xyz — direct APK, no Play Store, no account required.

### First Setup
After installing, configure an AI model to power the agent. Twent is fully model-agnostic:

**Cloud models:**
- OpenAI: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1, o3-mini, etc.
- Claude: claude-sonnet-4, claude-opus-4, claude-3-5-sonnet
- Gemini: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.0-flash
- Deepseek: deepseek-chat, deepseek-coder
- OpenRouter: Any model on the OpenRouter platform
- Kilo Gateway: Kilo AI endpoints
- NVIDIA NIMS: NVIDIA NIM endpoints
- LM Studio: Local LM Studio server

**Local models:**
- GGUF files via MNN (Mobile Neural Networks)
- GGUF files via llama.cpp
- MNN model files (.mnn)

**Setup path:** Settings → Model & Parameters Configuration → [Provider] → Enter API key → Select model.

### Your First Chat
Open the chat screen and type a message. The AI can:
- Answer questions and explain concepts
- Run Linux commands in the Ubuntu terminal
- Read and write files on your device
- Browse the web and summarize pages
- Interact with any Android app (tap, swipe, type)
- Automate multi-step workflows

Every tool call appears as an expandable card showing tool name, parameters, and results. Full transparency — you see exactly what the AI is doing.

### Permissions
Twent uses Android permissions to access device capabilities.

**Required:**
- Storage: Read/write files on your device
- Network: API calls, web browsing, MCP connections

**Optional (Recommended):**
- Accessibility: UI automation (tap, swipe, read screen). The most powerful permission — enables the AI to control any app.
- Display Over Other Apps: Floating chat bubble, screen capture
- Media Projection: Screenshot capture for visual AI understanding

**Advanced:**
- ADB: System-level commands (pm, am, dumpsys)
- Root: Full system access (optional, not required for any core feature)

---

## Chat Features

### Chat Overview
The main chat interface. Key capabilities:
- **Streaming responses** — AI writes character-by-character, no waiting for full reply
- **Tool transparency** — Every tool call is visible with parameters and results
- **Conversation management** — Edit messages, branch conversations, lock threads, auto-summarize
- **Shared content** — Share files, links, screenshots from other apps into the conversation
- **Markdown rendering** — Code blocks, tables, LaTeX math, Mermaid diagrams

**Context window:** For long conversations, Twent auto-summarizes older messages to fit the model's context window. Configure in Settings → Context & Summary.

### Chat Styles
Two display modes:
- **Cursor style** — Character-by-character streaming with blinking cursor (like Claude/ChatGPT)
- **Bubble style** — Traditional chat bubbles with avatars

Customize: Wallpapers, light/dark theme colors, font sizes, message spacing, code block styling. Settings → Theme.

### Floating Chat
Use Twent in an overlay on top of any app. The AI can see your screen and interact with any application.

**Modes:**
- **Bubble** — Small draggable bubble that expands on tap
- **Window** — Resizable chat window
- **Fullscreen** — Full overlay, blocks other apps

**Use cases:**
- Ask AI for help with what's currently on screen
- Have AI fill out forms in any app
- Debug errors while reading logcat
- Translate text from any app in real time
- Trigger workflows without leaving your current app

---

## Tools (50+ Built-in Tools)

Twent's tools give the AI real capabilities on your device. Every tool call is transparent — you always see the exact parameters and results.

### Tool Categories

**File System:**
- `read_file(path, offset?, limit?)` — Read file contents with pagination
- `write_file(path, content)` — Write/create files (creates directories)
- `patch(path, old_string, new_string, replace_all?)` — Find-and-replace edits
- `search_files(pattern, target?, path?, file_glob?, limit?)` — Search inside files or find by name
- `compress/decompress` — ZIP, TAR, GZ operations

**File locations:**
- App data: `/data/data/com.ai.assistance.twent/`
- Internal storage: `/sdcard/` or `/storage/emulated/0/`
- Ubuntu environment: `~/` (inside built-in Linux)

**Shell & Terminal:**
- `terminal(command, timeout?, workdir?, background?, pty?)` — Execute Linux commands

Full Ubuntu 24.04 environment:
```
apt update && apt install python3-pip
pip install requests numpy pandas
git clone https://github.com/user/repo
cd repo && npm install && npm run build
ssh user@server.com
```

Package managers: apt, pip, npm, cargo, gem
Shells: bash, zsh, fish
Compilers: Python 3, Node.js, Go, Rust, C/C++
Tools: git, gh, SSH, vim, nano, curl, wget

**Web:**
- `fetch_url(url, max_length?, raw?, start_index?)` — Fetch page content as markdown
- `search(query, limit?, engine?)` — DuckDuckGo, Tavily, Bing, Scholar
- `download_file(url, path?)` — Download files to device
- `web_visit(url, question?)` — Built-in browser with JavaScript execution

**Media:**
- **FFmpeg:** Convert formats, extract audio, trim clips, merge files, extract frames, add subtitles
- **Speech-to-Text:** Sherpa-NCNN (local/offline), HTTP STT (cloud)
- **Text-to-Speech:** System TTS (built-in Android), OpenAI TTS (cloud), SiliconFlow TTS (cloud, Chinese voices)
- **Camera:** Capture photos and videos
- **OCR:** Extract text from images using built-in OCR

**UI Automation** (requires Accessibility permission):
- `tap(ref)` — Tap UI element by reference ID
- `swipe(startX, startY, endX, endY, duration?)` — Swipe gesture with coordinates
- `scroll(direction)` — Scroll up/down/left/right
- `type(text, ref?)` — Type text into a field
- `screenshot()` — Capture the current screen
- `get_ui_tree()` — Get full UI element hierarchy with ref IDs
- `press_key(key)` — Back, Home, Volume, Recents, etc.
- `open_app(packageName)` — Launch an app by package name

**Dual pipeline:** Twent uses both the Android Accessibility UI tree AND visual screenshots to understand your screen. Every tool call automatically captures screen context before executing.

**JavaScript:**
- `execute_code(code, lang?)` — Run Python code (built-in) or JavaScript (Node.js via shell)

Install npm packages via shell, then use them in scripts:
```bash
cd /data/data/com.ai.assistance.twent/files/js
npm install axios lodash
```

**MCP Tools:**
- Tools injected from MCP servers — the AI gets new capabilities by installing MCP plugins

### Parallel Execution
Read-only tools (file reads, network checks, device info) run in parallel automatically — faster responses without sequential waiting.

---

## MCP (Model Context Protocol)

MCP is a standard for connecting AI to external tools. Install an MCP server and the AI gains new capabilities instantly.

**Installation:**
- Browse: Plugins → MCP → Shop Icon
- Or add manually via JSON config

**Popular servers:**
- Filesystem: Extended file operations (walk directories, watch changes)
- Git: Repository management (commits, branches, PRs)
- SQLite: Query local databases
- Brave Search: Web search without leaving the phone
- Puppeteer: Browser automation on-device
- GitHub: Issues, PRs, repos
- Slack: Messaging and channels
- Notion: Pages and databases

The AI can install MCP servers autonomously when you ask it to — no manual configuration required. Just say "install the GitHub MCP server" and Twent handles it.

---

## Automation & Workflows

### Methods
1. **UI Automation** — Control any app by tapping/swiping/reading the UI tree
2. **Shell Commands** — Run Linux commands and scripts
3. **Intents** — Launch apps, send broadcasts
4. **Workflows** — Multi-step automations with triggers and conditions

### Workflow Types
- **Simple** — One trigger, one action (e.g., battery low → notify)
- **Chained** — Multiple actions in sequence (e.g., morning trigger → weather → calendar → briefing)
- **Conditional** — If/else branching (e.g., if WiFi connected, sync files, else skip)
- **Scheduled** — Run on cron schedule, interval, or time-of-day

### Trigger Types
- Schedule: Cron-based timing
- Interval: Every N minutes/hours
- App Launch: When an app opens
- Notification: On incoming notification
- Battery: On battery level change
- Wake Word: Voice trigger phrase

### Tasker Integration
Connect Twent with the Tasker automation app. Twent registers as a Tasker plugin — trigger Twent from Tasker profiles and vice versa.

**Setup:** Settings → Integrations → Tasker → Enable

**Example:** Battery < 20% → Tasker sends to Twent: "My battery is at 20%. Check what apps are draining it." Twent analyzes and gives recommendations.

---

## Packages & Skills

### Packages
Extension system for Twent — bundles tools, dependencies, and configuration into installable units.

**Structure:**
```
my-package/
├── package.json       # Metadata (name, version, description)
├── tools/index.ts     # Tool definitions
├── scripts/           # Helper scripts
└── assets/            # Static data
```

**Installing:**
- From marketplace: Settings → Packages → Marketplace
- Manual: Import ZIP or directory
- Via URL: The AI can install packages for you

### Skills
Pre-configured behavior bundles that teach the AI how to do specific tasks — includes system prompts, tools, workflows, and assets.

**How skills work:** When you ask something, Twent checks if any installed skill is relevant. If so, it loads that skill's prompts and tools to handle the request.

**Popular skills:**
- Code Review: Analyze code for bugs, security issues, and style
- Data Analysis: Process CSVs, generate charts, statistical summaries
- Writing Assistant: Editorial mode for blog posts, documentation, emails
- Research: Deep research on topics with web search and citation

### Marketplace
Browse and install community-built packages, skills, and MCP servers.

**Sections:**
- Packages — JS/TS tool bundles
- Skills — AI behavior bundles
- MCP Servers — External tool providers

**Publishing:** Share your creations via Settings → Packages → Publish. Items are stored and indexed automatically.

---

## Memory System

Persistent knowledge storage that survives across conversations. The AI remembers your preferences, past discussions, and important information.

**Memory types:**
- User Preferences — Communication style, preferred apps, contacts
- Conversation Summaries — Key decisions and action items
- Knowledge Base — Notes, reference material, research

**Memory tools:**
- `memory_search(query, limit?)` — Search across all memories
- `memory_save(content, target?)` — Save new information
- `memory_update(old_text, new_text)` — Update existing memories
- `memory_link(source_id, target_id, relationship)` — Create relationships between memories

All memories are stored locally on your device. Nothing leaves your device except API calls for processing.

---

## Voice & Speech

### Text-to-Speech Engines
- **System TTS:** Built-in Android TTS (varies by device)
- **OpenAI TTS:** Cloud, 50+ voices, high quality (requires API key)
- **SiliconFlow TTS:** Cloud, optimized for Chinese/English voices (requires API key)

### Speech-to-Text Engines
- **Sherpa-NCNN:** Local and offline (no internet required)
- **HTTP STT:** Cloud-based STT (requires API key)

### Wake Word
Set a custom trigger phrase in Settings → Speech Services → Wake Word. The device listens locally for the phrase and starts recording your command. Works offline.

### Hands-free Mode
Enable voice conversation mode and talk to your AI without touching your phone.

---

## Settings & Configuration

### Quick Setup
- Agent theme (light/dark/auto)
- Model configuration (provider, API key, model selection)
- System prompts and instructions

### Interface
- Theme: Colors, fonts, spacing, wallpapers, chat bubble style
- Display: Cursor vs bubble mode, message spacing
- Language: UI language
- Layout: Chat width, sidebar

### Intelligence
- API usage settings (temperature, max tokens, top_p)
- Context window and summarization settings
- Memory retention period

### Data
- Chat session management
- Backup and restore
- Custom HTTP headers for API calls

### System
- Speech services (TTS/STT engine selection)
- Tool permissions (enable/disable specific tool categories)
- Power user mode (advanced features)
- ADB and root access toggles

### Model Configuration
Configure any supported model: OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Deepseek, OpenRouter, Kilo Gateway, NVIDIA NIMS, LM Studio, local GGUF/MNN. Set temperature, max tokens, and other parameters per model.

---

## Toolbox

Built-in utility screens for debugging, development, and system management.

### Development Tools
- **Shell Executor** — Run single commands directly
- **Terminal** — Full terminal emulator
- **File Manager** — Browse device files
- **SQL Viewer** — Query SQLite databases on device

### System Tools
- **Logcat Viewer** — Android system logs with filtering
- **UI Debugger** — Inspect UI hierarchy and element refs
- **Tool Tester** — Test individual tools in isolation

Access via Settings → Toolbox.

---

## Technical Specifications

| Specification | Value |
|---|---|
| Platform | Android 8.0+ (API 26) |
| Architecture | arm64-v8a |
| RAM | 3GB+ recommended |
| Storage | 500MB+ free |
| Root required | No |
| Terminal | Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (chroot container) |
| Package managers | apt, pip, npm, cargo, gem |
| Shells | bash, zsh, fish |
| Languages | Python 3, Node.js, Go, Rust, C/C++ |
| Editors | vim, nano |
| Version control | git, gh CLI |
| Editors | vim, nano, VS Code Server |

### AI Model Support
- OpenAI (GPT-4o, o1, o3-mini, etc.)
- Anthropic (Claude 3.5, Claude 4)
- Google (Gemini 2.0, Gemini 2.5)
- Deepseek (deepseek-chat, deepseek-coder)
- OpenRouter (any model on platform)
- Kilo Gateway
- NVIDIA NIMS
- LM Studio (local server)
- Local GGUF models (MNN/llama.cpp)

---

## Privacy & Security

- All data stored locally on device
- API keys encrypted with Android KeyStore (BYOK — Bring Your Own Key)
- API keys never leave your device — encrypted locally before any API call
- Zero telemetry, zero analytics, no usage tracking
- No data collection of any kind
- Open source (core components on GitHub)
- Local AI models (MNN/llama.cpp) for fully offline inference
- On-device text embedding generation

---

## Quick Reference

### Download & Install
1. Go to https://twent.xyz
2. Download APK directly (no Play Store, no account)
3. Enable "Unknown sources" in Android Settings → Security
4. Install and launch — no setup wizard, no credit card

### Key URLs
- Website: https://twent.xyz
- Documentation: https://twent.xyz/docs
- Blog: https://twent.xyz/blog
- Changelog: https://twent.xyz/changelog
- Pricing: https://twent.xyz/pricing
- Support: support@twent.xyz

### Key Pages
- [Home](https://twent.xyz/) — Main landing
- [AI Agent for Developers](https://twent.xyz/ai-agent-for-developers) — Claude Code, Codex, SSH, Git on phone
- [Ubuntu Terminal](https://twent.xyz/terminal-on-android) — Full Linux in your pocket
- [Android Automation](https://twent.xyz/android-automation-power-user) — Auto-tap, swipe, AI scripts
- [Privacy-First AI](https://twent.xyz/privacy-first-ai-android) — BYOK, local models, zero telemetry
- [AI Marketplace](https://twent.xyz/ai-marketplace-creators) — Build and sell AI skills
- [Enterprise](https://twent.xyz/enterprise-ai-agent) — Team deployment with admin controls
